channel.title: The Car Jury, News
channel.description: News and verdicts on the Indian car market from The Car Jury.

item.title: Volkswagen's India Pipeline Is Thin and Late, and the Virtus Facelift Won't Fix That
item.description: Volkswagen has four cars lined up for India led by the Virtus facelift in Q3 2026. We say the plan is too cautious to move the needle.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Honda's India Partnership Hunt Is An Admission, Not A Strategy
item.description: Honda is openly seeking an India partner after selling just 4,069 cars in April 2026. The Car Jury's take on what this really signals.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: SBI's Rs 8,000 Crore Bet On JSW Motors Is The EV Industry's Real Stamp Of Arrival
item.description: SBI has committed Rs 8,000 crore to JSW Motors over a 10-year tenure. The Car Jury on what this means for MG buyers and India's EV race.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Renault-Nissan April 2026 Sales: Duster Does the Heavy Lifting, Nissan Still Stuck Below 1%
item.description: Renault and Nissan combined for 8,616 units in April 2026, up from 4,351 a year ago. The Duster restart is the only real story here.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Maruti's Up To Rs 30,000 June Hike: Book Now If You're Near The Showroom
item.description: Maruti Suzuki raises prices by up to Rs 30,000 across Arena and Nexa from June 2026. Our verdict: lock in your booking before month-end.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Mahindra Owns The 4.5-4.8m SUV Segment: Scorpio And XUV7XO Bury The Competition In April 2026
item.description: Mahindra Scorpio and XUV7XO took 63 percent of the 4.5-4.8m SUV segment in April 2026. Tata, Toyota and MG have a problem.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: 2026 Honda City Facelift Is Still The Thinking Sedan Buyer's Best Bet At Rs 12-21 Lakh
item.description: The 2026 Honda City facelift starts at Rs 12 lakh with the ZX+ e:HEV hybrid at Rs 21 lakh. TCJ's verdict on whether the variant spread makes sense.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Mid-Size SUV Sales April 2026: Creta Holds, Victoris Storms In, Sierra Arrives
item.description: Mid-size SUV sales jumped 72% YoY in April 2026 as Creta led, Victoris debuted at No.2 and Sierra entered. The Car Jury's verdict on what to buy.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Next Creta With Pleos Connect: Hyundai Is About To Out-Tech Every Mid-Size SUV
item.description: The 3rd-gen Hyundai Creta spied with Pleos Connect single-screen cockpit signals a tech lead over Seltos, Taigun and Grand Vitara in India.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Honda ZR-V e:HEV Is Honda's Premium Reset, But CBU Pricing Will Decide Its Fate
item.description: Honda ZR-V e:HEV bookings open in India with 22.8 km/l claimed mileage, Level-2 ADAS and CBU import route. The Car Jury's verdict on what it means.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: MG Cyberster Verdict: At Rs 77.49 Lakh, It's the Cheapest Way to Feel Like a Supercar Owner
item.description: MG Cyberster review verdict: at Rs 77.49 lakh ex-showroom, this electric convertible with scissor doors is India's best value drama buy.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Mercedes GLE and GLS Night Editions: Black Paint, Big Markup, Limited Logic
item.description: Mercedes GLE and GLS Night Editions launched in India from Rs 1.05 crore ex-showroom. The Car Jury's verdict on the cosmetic markup of up to Rs 5 lakh.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: 2027 Hyundai Creta Spied Again: Seltos Hybrid Hardware Is The Real Story
item.description: The 2027 Hyundai Creta has been spied in Jaipur sharing test hardware with the new Kia Seltos hybrid. Here is what it means for Indian buyers.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Nissan Tekton Debut Set for July 9: Duster Twin Walks Into India's Toughest Segment
item.description: Nissan Tekton, the badge-engineered Renault Duster, will debut in India on July 9, 2026 with petrol and hybrid options in the mid-size SUV segment.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: 2026 Mercedes-AMG GT 4-Door Coupe Goes Electric: 1,169 bhp, 2.1s to 100
item.description: The 2026 Mercedes-AMG GT 4-Door Coupe ditches the V8 for an 800V electric setup, with 1,169 bhp in GT 63 trim and 700 km of claimed range.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Tata Tiago Facelift Lands 28 May: Mild Refresh, Not a Reinvention
item.description: Tata confirms the 2026 Tiago facelift for 28 May with revised styling and more equipment. Here is what buyers should actually expect.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Maruti's Triple Test: YMC EV, Brezza and Baleno Facelifts Spied Together
item.description: Maruti's YMC electric MPV was spotted testing alongside the Brezza and Baleno facelifts, signalling a packed 2026-27 product run for the brand.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Maruti's Second Kharkhoda Plant Goes Live: Brezza, Victoris Output Hits Top Gear
item.description: Maruti Suzuki begins production at its second Kharkhoda plant, taking total India capacity to 26.5 lakh units annually across four facilities.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Ethanol Surplus Is Real, Flex-Fuel Cars Are Not: CareEdge Calls It
item.description: CareEdge says India's ethanol capacity is running at 60% utilisation and flex-fuel vehicle adoption will stay slow for at least three years.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Tata Sierra EV Confirmed For July-September 2026: The BE6 Finally Has A Rival
item.description: Tata confirms Sierra EV for Q2 FY2026-27, targeting under Rs 18 lakh and 450 km range to take on the Mahindra BE6. Here are the facts.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)
